title: Arthraxon lasiocoleus
taxonomy: Arthraxon lasiocoleus is a species of grass. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This species is further classified into the tribe Andropogoneae, within the subfamily Panicoideae. Arthraxon lasiocoleus is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. Its classification also includes the genus Arthraxon, which is a monotypic genus.

intro: Arthraxon lasiocoleus, also known as Japanese milkwort, is a perennial grass native to East Asia, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Arthraxon lasiocoleus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
